rationale: Both correctly identify PR #4792 as the fix for the #4527 regression and match the ground-truth thread resolution. Y is far more grounded, citing precise file:line references across the coordinator, scheduler mixin, and connector code, quoting exact log strings, and explaining the mechanism specific to multi-stage full-payload-consumer stages (matching the issue title) rather than just the generic runner change. Y is also more complete: it gives concrete next steps (upgrade/cherry-pick/workaround), flags red herrings in the user's environment dump, and offers a clear reopen path, whereas X's citations are approximate and it omits the architectural 'why multi-stage hangs' detail.
